title: pleiarina subserrata
intro: Pleiarina subserrata, also known as the 'Sandpaper bush', is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the family Rhamnaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems contain salicylic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including pain relief, wound healing, and antimicrobial activity. Pleiarina subserrata can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
